name: El Robledo — circular desde Porzuna
region_intro: Mountain biking around Porzuna offers diverse terrain in the Montes de Toledo region, north of Ciudad Real, Spain. The landscape features volcanic areas, including the Cerro Santos volcano, and mountainous zones like the Sierra de la Cruz de Mayo. Riders can explore trails through river valleys, such as the Bullaque River, and experience varied topography. This region provides a natural environment suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Porzuna The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Robledo — circular desde Porzuna, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path. This trail explores the countryside around Porzuna, featuring varied natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Matalasbestias – Ermita de San Isidro (Picón) loop from Porzuna, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through hilly terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Porzuna is defined by volcanic features, mountainous areas, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate ability levels, with routes featuring notable elevation changes. The routes in Porzuna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Porzuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Porzuna?
A: The region around Porzuna offers diverse terrain for mountain biking. You'll encounter volcanic landscapes, including areas near the unique Cerro Santos volcano, and mountainous zones like the Sierra de la Cruz de Mayo. Trails also wind through river valleys, such as the Bullaque River, providing varied topography. Expect a mix of challenging ascents and descents, with routes generally suited for moderate ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails starting from Porzuna?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186792689/el-robledo-circular-desde-porzuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Robledo — circular desde Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; trail is a moderate 28.1-mile (45.3 km) path exploring the countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186781868/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-from-circular-via-the-sendero-de-la-colada-de-navalrincon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 31.1 miles (50.1 km) and offers views of the reservoir.

Q: What are some scenic spots or viewpoints I can reach by mountain bike near Porzuna?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. The volcanic areas, particularly around Cerro Santos, offer panoramic views. The Sierra de la Cruz de Mayo also provides rewarding vistas after challenging ascents. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186781868/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-from-circular-via-the-sendero-de-la-colada-de-navalrincon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Porzuna?
A: The mountain bike trails around Porzuna are generally categorized as moderate, often featuring significant elevation changes. While there are no specific 'easy' trails listed in the guide data, families with older children or those with some mountain biking experience may find suitable sections. For very young children or beginners, it's advisable to check individual route profiles for elevation and technical difficulty before setting out.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Porzuna?
A: The Montes de Toledo region, where Porzuna is located, experiences hot summers and mild winters. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. During summer, it's best to ride early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalasbestias – Ermita de San Isidro (Picón) loop from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3565795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Isidro (Picón)&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might take you near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6772678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Teja Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colada de Navalrincón Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable feature in the area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Porzuna?
A: The mountain biking routes in Porzuna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes volcanic features, mountainous areas, and river valleys, providing a diverse and engaging experience. The network offers options for moderate ability levels, with routes featuring notable elevation changes that add to the challenge and reward.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails in Porzuna?
A: Porzuna is a rural town, and while there might be local bus services connecting it to larger towns like Ciudad Real, direct public transport specifically to trailheads might be limited. It's generally recommended to have your own transport or arrange for local taxis to access the starting points of the mountain bike routes. One route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage (Porzuna) – Parish Church of Porzuna loop from Parada de Autobús de Las Rabinadas&lt;/a&gt;, mentions starting from a bus stop, suggesting some accessibility.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Porzuna area?
A: For general mountain biking on the trails around Porzuna, specific permits are typically not required. However, if you plan to venture into nearby protected areas like the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros or Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel (which are about 30 minutes away), it's advisable to check their official regulations, as some activities or routes within national parks might require prior authorization or have specific rules.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails in Porzuna?
A: Porzuna itself is a town with agricultural roots and offers local amenities. You can find restaurants and bars in Porzuna where you can refuel after your ride. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trails are uncommon in rural areas, the town provides options for refreshments and meals. Nearby towns may also offer additional choices.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through or near the Bullaque River Valley?
A: Yes, the Bullaque River Valley is a notable natural feature in the region, adding to the scenic beauty and offering additional routes for exploration. While specific routes are not detailed in the guide data, the general description of mountain biking around Porzuna mentions trails through river valleys, indicating that you can expect to encounter this picturesque area on some of the routes.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on the mountain bike trails around Porzuna?
A: The mountain bike trails around Porzuna feature notable elevation changes, contributing to their moderate difficulty rating. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalasbestias – Ermita de San Isidro (Picón) loop from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 723 meters (2,372 feet). Other routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Alcolea – CM-4109 loop from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; also involve significant climbing, around 673 meters (2,208 feet).
tips: With The pillar of unified love, Nychos wanted the skeleton, human and animal, to be the protagonist of the intervention. The fear of death is a very human characteristic, and the skeleton is the clear representation of it. However, the artist wanted to take advantage of his recent research and personal experiences on the physical body, consciousness and spirituality to decontextualize our concept of death. For the Austrian artist, there is no place for the fear of death since love is everything and infinite.

Porzuna was a Roman and Visigoth settlement, and it is from these civilizations that historical remains have been found. The first settlers of the region are probably from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic according to the archaeological remains found. Most of these can be found in the Ciudad Real museum.
Hermitage of the Christ of Veracruz, with the singularity that it is located in an already inactive volcano. (Cerro de los Santos Volcano)
Nice route between towns on the bullaque river
